<section id="mle-user-manual" class="mle-user-manual" aria-label="MyLottoExpert user manual">
  <details class="mle-user-manual__toggle">
    <summary class="mle-user-manual__open-button">
      <span class="mle-user-manual__cta-copy">
        <span class="mle-user-manual__open-eyebrow">Guide</span>
        <span class="mle-user-manual__open-title">Start Here: How to Use MyLottoExpert</span>
        <span class="mle-user-manual__open-text">New to this page? Click anywhere on this blue panel to open the full guide. It explains what each section means, what to click next, when to wait, when not to delete runs, and how LottoExpert narrows in two steps: first by finding the best history window, then by testing one SKAI setting at a time.</span>
        <span class="mle-user-manual__precision-proof">
          <strong>Why Precision Focus matters</strong>
          <span>SKAI has about 7,033,509,659,011,409,510,400,000,000,000 visible AI setting combinations. That is about 7.03 nonillion combinations. Precision Focus matters because you cannot test that by guessing. LottoExpert narrows the search by keeping valid evidence, finding repeated settings, locking only what repeats, and testing one setting at a time.</span>
        </span>
      </span>
      <span class="mle-user-manual__cta-action" aria-hidden="true">        <span class="mle-user-manual__cta-open-text">Open Guide</span>
        <span class="mle-user-manual__cta-close-text">Close Guide</span>
      </span>
    </summary>
    <div class="mle-user-manual__shell">
      <div class="mle-user-manual__hero">
        <p class="mle-user-manual__eyebrow">Complete Guide</p>
        <h2 class="mle-user-manual__title">How to Use MyLottoExpert</h2>
        <p class="mle-user-manual__intro">Use this guide when you are not sure what a section means or what to do next. The goal is simple: run SKAI, save the results, let LottoExpert score them automatically after each draw, and let MyLottoExpert tell you the next controlled test. First it looks for the best history window. Then it narrows one setting at a time.</p>
      </div>

      <div class="mle-user-manual__body">
        <div class="mle-user-manual__notice">
          <strong>Truth-based strategy:</strong> Lottery drawings are random, but your process does not have to be. MyLottoExpert does not guarantee wins; it helps you organize saved runs, score completed draws, compare results against a random baseline, and narrow toward better-supported SKAI settings over time.
        </div>

        <div class="mle-user-manual__quick-grid">
          <div class="mle-user-manual__quick-card"><strong>If you are new</strong><span>Open one lottery card, read Strong Evidence Runs, check Keep Locked, then follow Test Next.</span></div>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__quick-card"><strong>Main rule</strong><span>Keep valid evidence. Do not delete every weak run. Exclude noisy runs from advice instead.</span></div>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__quick-card"><strong>Main goal</strong><span>Narrow toward settings that repeat across strong saved and scored evidence.</span></div>
        </div>

        <details open>
          <summary>1. Start here if this is your first time</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>If this is your first time using MyLottoExpert, do not try to understand every number on the page at once. Start with one lottery card and follow the simple path below.</p>
            <ol>
              <li>Pick one lottery card that you care about.</li>
              <li>Open the card and read the top summary.</li>
              <li>Look at Recommendation Level and Precision Progress.</li>
              <li>Open Strong Evidence Runs to see which saved runs performed best.</li>
              <li>Open Settings That Repeat to see which settings show up more than once.</li>
              <li>Read Keep Locked, Test Next, and Not Proven Yet.</li>
              <li>Do not change everything at once.</li>
              <li>Follow the amber action only when the page says the lottery is ready for that step.</li>
              <li>After the next draw is complete, come back and let LottoExpert score the result.</li>
            </ol>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__callout">Simple version: pick one lottery, follow the amber action, score the draw, and let MyLottoExpert move from regular evidence to History Window and then Settings Precision.</div>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>2. What this page is for</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>MyLottoExpert is your lottery decision workspace. It brings together saved predictions, saved settings, saved numbers, completed draw results, random-baseline edge checks, visual comparisons, evidence scores, History Window guidance, Settings Precision guidance, and SKAI batch recommendations.</p>
            <p>The page is trying to answer this question for each lottery:</p>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__callout">What happened last draw, how did the predictions compare, what phase is this lottery in, and what should be tested next?</div>
            <p>Each lottery card is separate. Fantasy 5 should use Fantasy 5 evidence only. EuroMillions should use EuroMillions evidence only. Powerball should use Powerball evidence only.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>3. The big idea: Precision Focus</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Precision Focus is the overall process of moving from broad testing to tighter, better-supported decisions. MyLottoExpert does this in stages: first by checking the History Window Range, then by testing one SKAI setting inside the Settings Precision Range.</p>
            <p>At the beginning, you may test several methods and settings. Over time, LottoExpert studies saved and scored runs to see which settings keep showing up when results are stronger. Those repeated settings become clues.</p>
            <h4>What Precision Focus means</h4>
            <p>It means the evidence is starting to point toward a narrower area that deserves controlled testing, either the history window or one SKAI setting.</p>
            <h4>What Precision Focus does not mean</h4>
            <p>It does not mean the lottery is solved. It does not mean a result is guaranteed. It does not mean every setting from one good run should be locked.</p>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__warning">Precision Focus is a narrowing process, not a guarantee. The goal is stronger consistency, better evidence, and smarter next tests: first the history window, then one setting at a time.</div>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>4. What if the page says Not Ready or Needs More Evidence?</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>If the page says Not Ready, Too Early, Learning Stage, or Needs More Evidence, nothing is broken. It means LottoExpert does not have enough saved and scored evidence for that lottery yet.</p>
            <h4>What to do</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>Save more meaningful runs for that lottery.</li>
              <li>Wait until the official draw is complete.</li>
              <li>Come back after the draw so the page can compare predictions to results.</li>
              <li>Do not force a controlled 9-run batch if the page says the evidence is not ready. Run regular SKAI batches first when the amber action says to build evidence.</li>
            </ul>
            <h4>Why this matters</h4>
            <p>A recommendation based on one lucky result can be misleading. LottoExpert needs repeated evidence before it should ask you to lock settings.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>5. Why weak runs should not always be deleted</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Weak runs can still teach the system what not to repeat. If you delete every weak run, LottoExpert may lose useful contrast between what worked and what did not work.</p>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__callout">Best rule: keep valid evidence, but control whether it affects advice.</div>
            <h4>Delete only when</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>The run was saved by mistake.</li>
              <li>The run belongs to the wrong lottery.</li>
              <li>The record is corrupted.</li>
              <li>The row is a duplicate error.</li>
              <li>The run was test junk that you truly do not want stored.</li>
            </ul>
            <h4>Do not delete only because</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>The run was weak but valid.</li>
              <li>The run teaches what settings did not help.</li>
              <li>The run belongs to a real completed draw.</li>
              <li>You may want to compare it later.</li>
            </ul>
            <p>If a run is real but should not guide advice, use Exclude from Advice.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>6. Evidence status actions: what each button means</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Evidence status controls whether a saved run should guide future recommendations.</p>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__table-wrap">
              <table>
                <thead><tr><th>Status</th><th>What it means</th><th>When to use it</th></tr></thead>
                <tbody>
                  <tr><td>Use for Future Advice</td><td>This run can guide recommendations, Precision Focus, Current Locked Settings, and SKAI batches.</td><td>Use for strong, clean, useful evidence.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Keep for Review</td><td>This run stays visible for history, but LottoExpert should not rely on it strongly.</td><td>Use for uncertain evidence that you still want to see.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Exclude from Advice</td><td>This run stays stored if supported, but it does not guide Precision Focus or recommendations.</td><td>Use for noisy, experimental, weak, or questionable runs.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Delete</td><td>This removes the run according to the page rules.</td><td>Use for mistakes, duplicates, wrong lottery records, corrupted rows, or records you truly want removed.</td></tr>
                </tbody>
              </table>
            </div>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__warning">When in doubt, use Keep for Review or Exclude from Advice. Do not delete unless the record should truly be removed.</div>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>7. Strong Evidence Runs versus Keep Locked settings</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>This is one of the most important concepts on the page.</p>
            <h4>Strong Evidence Runs</h4>
            <p>Strong Evidence Runs are saved completed runs that performed well. A run may be strong evidence if it found all expected main numbers, had high main hits, strong extra-ball results, low missing count, strong top10 results, strong top20 results, or a strong average rank.</p>
            <h4>Keep Locked settings</h4>
            <p>Keep Locked settings are settings that repeat across strong evidence. These are the settings LottoExpert wants to keep steady during the next controlled test.</p>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__callout">A run can be strong evidence even if not every setting from that run is ready to lock.</div>
            <p>If only 2 or 3 settings are locked, that does not mean the other evidence was thrown away. It means only those settings repeated clearly enough to keep steady.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>8. What does All Main Numbers Found mean?</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>All Main Numbers Found means the saved prediction set contained all of the main winning numbers for that completed draw.</p>
            <p>For Fantasy 5, that means all 5 main numbers were found in the predicted set. For Powerball, Mega Millions, and EuroMillions, main numbers and extra balls are tracked separately.</p>
            <h4>Why it matters</h4>
            <p>All Main Numbers Found is high-value evidence if the run is valid, saved, tied to the correct lottery, tied to a completed draw, not deleted, and not excluded from advice.</p>
            <h4>What it does not mean</h4>
            <p>It does not mean every setting from that run should be locked. LottoExpert keeps the run as strong evidence, then checks whether the same settings repeat across other strong runs.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>9. How to read a lottery card</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Each lottery card is one workspace for one lottery. Everything inside that card should belong only to that lottery.</p>
            <h4>Recommended reading order</h4>
            <ol>
              <li>Read the top summary.</li>
              <li>Check Recommendation Level.</li>
              <li>Check Precision Progress.</li>
              <li>Read Precision Focus.</li>
              <li>Open Strong Evidence Runs.</li>
              <li>Open Settings That Repeat.</li>
              <li>Review Current Locked Settings.</li>
              <li>Read Keep Locked, Test Next, and Not Proven Yet.</li>
              <li>Decide whether to create one recommended run or the 9-run SKAI batch.</li>
            </ol>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>10. Recommendation Level</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Recommendation Level tells you how mature the evidence is for one lottery.</p>
            <div class="mle-user-manual__table-wrap">
              <table>
                <thead><tr><th>Level</th><th>Meaning</th><th>What to do</th></tr></thead>
                <tbody>
                  <tr><td>Learning Stage</td><td>0-2 completed draw cycles. The lottery is still collecting evidence.</td><td>Run regular SKAI predictions or regular 9-combo batches and save them.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Early Direction</td><td>3+ draw cycles and 18+ scored runs. A possible direction may be forming.</td><td>Keep running regular evidence and do not overreact yet.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Exploratory Precision Batch</td><td>5+ draw cycles, 45+ scored runs, and repeated settings support.</td><td>Create the controlled 9-run Precision Focus batch only when the card says it is ready.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Narrowing Signal</td><td>6+ draw cycles and 45+ scored runs with repeated direction.</td><td>Keep the strongest settings stable and test one setting at a time.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Validated Focus Range</td><td>12+ draw cycles and 75+ scored runs.</td><td>Use the range as disciplined guidance while continuing to validate.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Settings Precision Range</td><td>20+ draw cycles and 120+ scored runs.</td><td>Use as the strongest current settings range, still without treating it as a guarantee.</td></tr>
                </tbody>
              </table>
            </div>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>11. Precision Progress</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Precision Progress shows where this lottery is in the learning process. It is not a win probability. It is a guide to evidence maturity.</p>
            <h4>Progress moves forward when</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>There are more completed draw cycles for the same lottery.</li>
              <li>There are more saved scored runs attached to those completed draw cycles.</li>
              <li>About 5 regular batch cycles build the first stronger foundation for a controlled Precision Focus batch.</li>
              <li>Strong Evidence Runs begin to repeat similar settings and show lift versus the random baseline.</li>
              <li>Current Locked Settings become reliable.</li>
              <li>Test Next becomes clear.</li>
            </ul>
            <h4>Progress should not jump forward from</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>One lucky result.</li>
              <li>Runs from another lottery.</li>
              <li>Deleted runs.</li>
              <li>Excluded runs.</li>
              <li>Unsaved-only history that cannot be tied to active saved evidence.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>12. Precision Focus section</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>The Precision Focus section shows the current setting range or setting area that LottoExpert is watching for this lottery.</p>
            <h4>What to look for</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>The setting family being tested, such as blend, window size, or draws analyzed.</li>
              <li>The current range.</li>
              <li>The evidence reason.</li>
              <li>What should stay locked.</li>
              <li>What should be tested next.</li>
            </ul>
            <p>The range should be based only on saved and scored runs for this lottery.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>13. Strong Evidence Runs section</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Strong Evidence Runs shows the saved completed runs that performed best.</p>
            <h4>What to check</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>Draw date.</li>
              <li>Main hits.</li>
              <li>Extra hits when the lottery has extra balls.</li>
              <li>Total hits.</li>
              <li>Missing count.</li>
              <li>Top10 and Top20 hits if available.</li>
              <li>Settings summary.</li>
              <li>Evidence status.</li>
            </ul>
            <p>Use this section to understand which saved runs deserve attention. Then use Settings That Repeat to see what those runs have in common.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>14. Settings That Repeat section</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Settings That Repeat shows which settings appear again and again across strong saved evidence.</p>
            <h4>Examples of repeated settings</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>Same method or source.</li>
              <li>Similar blend range.</li>
              <li>Same mode.</li>
              <li>Similar window size.</li>
              <li>Similar draws analyzed.</li>
              <li>Best Window on or off repeating.</li>
              <li>Auto Adjust on or off repeating.</li>
            </ul>
            <p>If a setting appears in only one good run, it is not proven yet. It may be promising, but it should not be locked until it repeats.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>15. Current Locked Settings</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Current Locked Settings are the current best-supported baseline for this lottery.</p>
            <p>If a lock exists, future recommended SKAI batches should use it as the control run. If no lock exists, LottoExpert needs more repeated evidence before saving a baseline.</p>
            <h4>How to use it</h4>
            <ol>
              <li>Read the evidence reason.</li>
              <li>Check which settings are locked.</li>
              <li>Do not change those settings during the next clean test.</li>
              <li>Use Test Next as the one setting to change.</li>
            </ol>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>16. Keep Locked, Test Next, and Not Proven Yet</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <h4>Keep Locked</h4>
            <p>These settings repeat across stronger evidence. Keep them steady so the next test is clean.</p>
            <h4>Test Next</h4>
            <p>This is the one setting LottoExpert wants to change next. Changing one setting at a time helps show what actually helped.</p>
            <h4>Not Proven Yet</h4>
            <p>These settings may matter, but they do not have enough repeated evidence yet.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>17. Recommended Next Settings Run</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Recommended Next Settings Run creates one clean test.</p>
            <p>LottoExpert copies the strongest current setup for this lottery, changes only one setting, and saves a new test run. After the next draw, you can compare whether that one change helped.</p>
            <h4>Use this when</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>You want the simplest next action.</li>
              <li>You do not want to run a full 9-run batch.</li>
              <li>You want to learn whether one setting helped.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>18. Controlled SKAI Batch Test</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>A recommended SKAI 9-run batch is a controlled test. It should be created only when the evidence supports it.</p>
            <h4>How it works</h4>
            <ul>
              <li>Run 1 is the control.</li>
              <li>If Current Locked Settings exist, Run 1 uses those settings.</li>
              <li>Runs 2 through 9 test one setting at different values inside the current range.</li>
              <li>All other settings stay the same.</li>
            </ul>
            <h4>Why it may not create</h4>
            <p>If LottoExpert cannot create eight useful distinct test values, or if the evidence is not repeated enough, it should refuse the batch instead of creating a fake one.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>19. What happens when you click each major button?</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <div class="mle-user-manual__table-wrap">
              <table>
                <thead><tr><th>Button or action</th><th>What happens</th><th>What to expect</th></tr></thead>
                <tbody>
                  <tr><td>Save as Current Locked Settings</td><td>Saves the current repeated baseline for this lottery.</td><td>Future SKAI batches can use it as the control.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Run Settings Precision 9-Run Batch</td><td>Creates one control run and eight one-setting test runs.</td><td>Use after the History Window phase is supported, so one SKAI setting can be tested cleanly.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Create Controlled SKAI Batch Test</td><td>Saves 9 controlled SKAI runs if the lottery is ready.</td><td>When enough evidence exists, LottoExpert may recommend a History Window batch to test 25 to 1,000 draws back, or a Settings Precision batch to test one SKAI setting inside its current range.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Use for Future Advice</td><td>Allows the run to guide recommendations.</td><td>The run can influence evidence, locks, and batches.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Keep for Review</td><td>Keeps the run visible without relying on it strongly.</td><td>Useful for history and comparison.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Exclude from Advice</td><td>Stops the run from guiding recommendations.</td><td>The run stays stored if supported, but does not influence Precision Focus.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Delete</td><td>Removes the run according to the page rules.</td><td>Use only for mistakes or records you truly want removed.</td></tr>
                </tbody>
              </table>
            </div>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>20. Visual Run Comparison</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Visual Run Comparison shows predicted balls compared with actual draw balls.</p>
            <h4>How to use it</h4>
            <ol>
              <li>Look at the best run first.</li>
              <li>Check main hits.</li>
              <li>Check extra hits when the lottery uses extra balls.</li>
              <li>Look for repeated settings across better runs.</li>
              <li>Do not judge the whole system from one row.</li>
            </ol>
            <p>For lotteries with extra balls, main balls and extra balls are separate. EuroMillions has Lucky Stars. Powerball has a Powerball. Mega Millions has a Mega Ball.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>21. Saved Predictions, Saved Numbers, and Saved Settings</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <h4>Saved Predictions</h4>
            <p>These are prediction runs you saved. They can become evidence after the draw is complete and scored.</p>
            <h4>Saved Numbers to Play</h4>
            <p>These are numbers you saved for playing or review.</p>
            <h4>Saved Settings</h4>
            <p>These are settings profiles you saved. They matter when they can be tied to strong repeated results.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>22. Favorite Lotteries and Favorite Wheeling Systems</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <h4>Favorite Lotteries</h4>
            <p>This section keeps your favorite lotteries easy to access. It may show logos, draw information, and next draw details.</p>
            <h4>Favorite Wheeling Systems</h4>
            <p>This section keeps your saved favorite wheeling systems easy to find. A wheeling system helps organize number combinations based on a chosen structure.</p>
            <p>These are global sections at the bottom of the page. They are not the same as the lottery-specific evidence inside each lottery card.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>23. A complete example workflow</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>Example: you are tracking Fantasy 5.</p>
            <ol>
              <li>You save several Fantasy 5 runs.</li>
              <li>After each draw, LottoExpert scores the saved runs.</li>
              <li>Several runs show strong results, including all 5 main numbers found.</li>
              <li>LottoExpert marks those as Strong Evidence Runs.</li>
              <li>The page checks which settings repeat across those strong runs.</li>
              <li>The repeated settings become Keep Locked.</li>
              <li>One uncertain setting becomes Test Next.</li>
              <li>You follow the amber action: regular SKAI, History Window, or Settings Precision.</li>
              <li>After the next draw, LottoExpert scores the new evidence.</li>
              <li>If the tested window or setting keeps helping, it may become part of the current supported setup.</li>
            </ol>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>24. What if you disagree with LottoExpert?</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <p>You can disagree with the recommendation. The best response is not to delete everything. Use the evidence controls carefully.</p>
            <ul>
              <li>Use Keep for Review if you want to watch the run but not rely on it strongly.</li>
              <li>Use Exclude from Advice if the run is real but should not guide Precision Focus.</li>
              <li>Use Delete only if the run should be removed.</li>
              <li>Continue testing one setting at a time.</li>
            </ul>
            <p>If you believe a run is important, keep it as valid evidence and let future scored draws confirm whether the pattern repeats.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>25. Troubleshooting common questions</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <h4>Why is my lottery not showing?</h4>
            <p>The card usually appears when there is active saved evidence for that lottery. Save a prediction or setting for that lottery and check again.</p>
            <h4>Why does LottoExpert say Not Ready?</h4>
            <p>There is not enough saved scored evidence yet, or the strong runs do not repeat the same settings clearly enough.</p>
            <h4>Why are only 2 or 3 settings locked?</h4>
            <p>Only those settings repeated clearly. Other settings may still be useful, but they are not proven enough to lock.</p>
            <h4>Why did the 9-run batch not create?</h4>
            <p>The page may not have enough repeated evidence, or it may not be able to create eight useful distinct test values.</p>
            <h4>Why does a weak run still appear?</h4>
            <p>Weak runs can be useful for review. If you do not want one guiding advice, use Exclude from Advice.</p>
            <h4>Why are extra balls separate?</h4>
            <p>Some lotteries draw main numbers and extra balls separately. LottoExpert tracks them separately so the score is clearer.</p>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>26. Common mistakes to avoid</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <ul>
              <li>Do not delete every weak run.</li>
              <li>Do not lock every setting from one good run.</li>
              <li>Do not judge the system from one draw only.</li>
              <li>Do not mix evidence from different lotteries.</li>
              <li>Do not change many settings at once when trying to learn cleanly.</li>
              <li>Do not treat Precision Focus as a guarantee.</li>
              <li>Do not ignore all-main-number runs when they are valid.</li>
              <li>Do not let deleted or excluded runs guide recommendations.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </details>

        <details>
          <summary>27. Quick glossary</summary>
          <div class="mle-user-manual__section">
            <div class="mle-user-manual__table-wrap">
              <table>
                <thead><tr><th>Term</th><th>Simple meaning</th></tr></thead>
                <tbody>
                  <tr><td>Saved run</td><td>A prediction or settings run you saved for tracking or learning.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Checked run</td><td>A saved run that has been compared to an official completed draw.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Strong Evidence Run</td><td>A saved scored run that performed well enough to be important evidence.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Settings That Repeat</td><td>Settings that appear again and again across stronger runs.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Keep Locked</td><td>Settings to keep steady because they repeat across strong evidence.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Test Next</td><td>The next controlled test: regular evidence, History Window, or one Settings Precision variable.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Not Proven Yet</td><td>Settings that may matter, but need more evidence before being trusted.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Current Locked Settings</td><td>The current best-supported baseline for this lottery.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Precision Focus</td><td>The overall narrowing process: regular evidence first, then History Window, then Settings Precision.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>9-run SKAI batch</td><td>One control run plus eight test runs. A History Window batch tests draw-history windows. A Settings Precision batch tests one SKAI setting.</td></tr>
                  <tr><td>Evidence score</td><td>A guide for ranking evidence quality. It is not a win probability.</td></tr>
                </tbody>
              </table>
            </div>
          </div>
        </details>

        <div class="mle-user-manual__final">
          <p><strong>Simple rule to remember:</strong> Keep valid evidence, highlight the strongest runs, lock only settings that repeat, test one setting at a time, and come back after each completed draw so LottoExpert can learn from the result.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </details>
</section>

$__mleBriefCards = array();
$__mleBriefTotals = array('active'=>0,'pending'=>0,'scored'=>0,'draw_cycles'=>0,'highest_stage'=>'Learning Stage','highest_stage_rank'=>0,'highest_stage_lottery'=>'');
$__mleBriefCleanLotteryName = static function($name) {
    $name = trim((string)$name);
    if ($name === '') { return ''; }
    $name = html_entity_decode($name, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8');
    $name = preg_replace('/\s+/', ' ', $name);
    $parts = preg_split('/\s+(?:-|\||\/)\s+/', $name);
    if (is_array($parts) && count($parts) > 1) {
        $last = trim((string)end($parts));
        if ($last !== '') { return $last; }
    }
    foreach (array('United States', 'Multi-State', 'Multi-Country', 'Lottery') as $prefix) {
        if (stripos($name, $prefix . ' ') === 0) {
            $name = trim(substr($name, strlen($prefix)));
        }
    }
    return $name;
};
$__mleBriefStageOrder = array(
    'learning_stage'=>0,
    'early_direction'=>1,
    'early_signal'=>1,
    'exploratory_batch'=>2,
    'narrowing_signal'=>3,
    'validated_focus_range'=>4,
    'precision_focus_range'=>5
);
$__mleBriefSourceCards = (array)(!empty($__mleAdvSelectorCards) ? $__mleAdvSelectorCards : $__mleAdvCards);
if (!empty($__mleAdvCards)) {
    foreach ((array)$__mleAdvCards as $__mleBriefFullCard) {
        if (!is_array($__mleBriefFullCard) || empty($__mleBriefFullCard)) { continue; }
        $__mleBriefFullCard = mylottoexpertSyncCardPendingCount($__mleBriefFullCard, $db, $workspaceUserId);
        $__mleBriefFullLid = (int)($__mleBriefFullCard['lottery_id'] ?? 0);
        if ($__mleBriefFullLid <= 0) { continue; }
        $__mleBriefDidReplace = false;
        foreach ($__mleBriefSourceCards as $__mleBriefSrcIdx => $__mleBriefSrcCard) {
            if ((int)($__mleBriefSrcCard['lottery_id'] ?? 0) === $__mleBriefFullLid) {
                $__mleBriefSourceCards[$__mleBriefSrcIdx] = $__mleBriefFullCard;
                $__mleBriefDidReplace = true;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (!$__mleBriefDidReplace) { $__mleBriefSourceCards[] = $__mleBriefFullCard; }
    }
    unset($__mleBriefFullCard, $__mleBriefFullLid, $__mleBriefDidReplace, $__mleBriefSrcIdx, $__mleBriefSrcCard);
}
/* [[MLE_V5_STRICT_PENDING_MAP_2026_06_01]]
   Build a strict DB-only pending map keyed by lottery_id for all visible top
   cards. This map is the authoritative source for both per-card pending counts
   and the global $__mleBriefTotals['pending'] total.

   Rules enforced:
   - Source is #__user_saved_numbers (real saved runs only).
   - Requires a valid upcoming draw date for the lottery; 0 pending otherwise.
   - Does NOT fall back to selector prediction_summary.pending_runs.
   - Passes $requireIdentity = true: each counted row must carry at least one
     of run_uuid, batch_uuid, source_batch_id, source_batch_run_id,
     prediction_target_id, or prediction_target_key. This prevents false
     pending counts from bucket-summary or advisory-aggregate rows.
   - strategy_set source rows are excluded.
   - When the DB query returns null (connection unavailable), pending is 0. */
$__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId = array();
$__mleStrictPendingScopeByLotteryId = array();
$__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId = array();
foreach ((array)$__mleAdvCards as $__mlePendingFullCardRaw) {
    $__mlePendingFullLid = (int)($__mlePendingFullCardRaw['lottery_id'] ?? 0);
    if ($__mlePendingFullLid <= 0) { continue; }
    $__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId[$__mlePendingFullLid] = (array)$__mlePendingFullCardRaw;
}
foreach ((array)$__mleBriefSourceCards as $__mlePendingCardRaw) {
    $__mlePendingLid = (int)($__mlePendingCardRaw['lottery_id'] ?? 0);
    if ($__mlePendingLid <= 0) { continue; }
    $__mlePendingScopeCard = (array)$__mlePendingCardRaw;
    if (empty($__mlePendingScopeCard['game_id']) && !empty($__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid]['game_id'])) {
        $__mlePendingScopeCard['game_id'] = (string)$__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid]['game_id'];
    }
    $____mlePendingScope = mylottoexpertResolveUpcomingDrawScope($__mlePendingScopeCard);
    if (trim((string)($____mlePendingScope['date'] ?? '')) === '' && !empty($__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid])) {
        $____mlePendingScope = mylottoexpertResolveUpcomingDrawScope((array)$__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid]);
    }
    $__mlePendingDate = trim((string)($____mlePendingScope['date'] ?? ''));
    if ($__mlePendingDate === '' || !preg_match('/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/', $__mlePendingDate)) {
        /* No valid upcoming draw date resolved: strict pending is 0.
           Do not fall back to prediction_summary.pending_runs from the selector
           card because that value is produced by mleAdvisorySummarizeActive-
           LotteryBucket() without a saved-run identity requirement and can
           produce false 1-pending counts. */
        $__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid] = 0;
        continue;
    }
    $__mlePendingDrawAt = trim((string)($____mlePendingScope['draw_at'] ?? ''));
    $__mlePendingScopeCard['next_draw_date'] = $__mlePendingDate;
    $__mlePendingScopeCard['target_draw_date'] = $__mlePendingDate;
    if ($__mlePendingDrawAt !== '' && strncmp($__mlePendingDrawAt, '0000-00-00', 10) !== 0) {
        $__mlePendingScopeCard['next_draw_at'] = $__mlePendingDrawAt;
        $__mlePendingScopeCard['target_draw_at'] = $__mlePendingDrawAt;
    }
    $__mlePendingCount = 0;
    if (is_object($db) && (int)$workspaceUserId > 0) {
        $__mlePendingDbResult = mylottoexpertDatabasePendingRunCount($db, $workspaceUserId, $__mlePendingScopeCard, true);
        if ($__mlePendingDbResult !== null) {
            $__mlePendingCount = (int)$__mlePendingDbResult;
        }
    }
    $__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid] = max(0, $__mlePendingCount);
    $__mleStrictPendingScopeByLotteryId[$__mlePendingLid] = array('date'=>$__mlePendingDate, 'draw_at'=>$__mlePendingDrawAt);
}
unset($__mlePendingFullCardRaw, $__mlePendingFullLid, $__mlePendingCardRaw, $__mlePendingLid, $__mlePendingScopeCard, $____mlePendingScope, $__mlePendingDate, $__mlePendingDrawAt, $__mlePendingCount, $__mlePendingDbResult);
foreach ((array)$__mleBriefSourceCards as $__briefCardRaw) {
    $__briefLid = (int)($__briefCardRaw['lottery_id'] ?? 0);
    if ($__briefLid <= 0) { continue; }
    $__briefName = trim((string)($__briefCardRaw['lottery_name'] ?? ''));
    if ($__briefName === '') { $__briefName = 'Lottery #' . $__briefLid; }
    $__briefCleanName = $__mleBriefCleanLotteryName($__briefName);
    if ($__briefCleanName === '') { $__briefCleanName = $__briefName; }
    $__briefStats = (array)($__briefCardRaw['stats'] ?? array());
    $__briefSummary = (array)($__briefCardRaw['prediction_summary'] ?? array());
    $__briefVisual = (array)($__briefCardRaw['visual_runs'] ?? array('upcoming'=>array()));
    $__briefProgress = (array)($__briefCardRaw['precision_progress'] ?? array());
    $__briefBatch = (array)($__briefCardRaw['recommended_skai_batch_test'] ?? array());
    $__briefSettings = (array)($__briefCardRaw['settings_advice'] ?? array());
    $__briefTopMethod = (array)($__briefCardRaw['top_method'] ?? array());
    $__briefMethodKey = mleAdvisorySourceToMethodKey((string)($__briefTopMethod['method'] ?? 'skai'));
    $__briefMethodLabel = mleAdvisoryMethodLabel($__briefMethodKey);
    $__briefMethodRole = mleAdvisoryMethodRoleLabel($__briefMethodKey);
    $__briefMethodTrack = mleAdvisoryMethodTrackLabel($__briefMethodKey);
    if (isset($__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId[$__briefLid])) {
        /* [[MLE_V5_STRICT_PENDING_ASSIGN_2026_06_01]]
           Use the strict DB-only count from $__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId.
           selector prediction_summary.pending_runs is NOT used here. */
        $__briefPending = max(0, (int)$__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId[$__briefLid]);
        if (!isset($__briefCardRaw['prediction_summary']) || !is_array($__briefCardRaw['prediction_summary'])) {
            $__briefCardRaw['prediction_summary'] = array();
        }
        if (!isset($__briefCardRaw['precision_progress']) || !is_array($__briefCardRaw['precision_progress'])) {
            $__briefCardRaw['precision_progress'] = array();
        }
        $__briefCardRaw['prediction_summary']['pending_runs'] = $__briefPending;
        $__briefCardRaw['precision_progress']['pending_runs'] = $__briefPending;
        $__briefCardRaw['mle_canonical_pending_runs'] = $__briefPending;
    } else {
        /* Lottery was not included in the strict pending map build (unexpected).
           Use 0 as a safe default. Do not fall back to selector pending_runs. */
        $__briefPending = 0;
        $__briefCardRaw = mylottoexpertSyncCardPendingCount((array)$__briefCardRaw, $db, $workspaceUserId);
    }
    $__briefSummary = (array)($__briefCardRaw['prediction_summary'] ?? array());
    $__briefVisual = (array)($__briefCardRaw['visual_runs'] ?? array('upcoming'=>array()));

    $__briefChecked = (int)($__briefSummary['scored_draws'] ?? ($__briefProgress['current_draw_cycles'] ?? ($__briefProgress['current_scored_runs'] ?? 0)));
    $__briefScored = (int)($__briefSummary['scored_runs'] ?? ($__briefSummary['scored_draws'] ?? ($__briefProgress['current_scored_runs'] ?? ($__briefStats['scored_runs'] ?? 0))));
    $__briefDrawCycles = (int)($__briefProgress['current_draw_cycles'] ?? ($__briefStats['completed_draws'] ?? 0));
    $__briefStageKey = (string)($__briefProgress['level_key'] ?? 'learning_stage');
    $__briefStageLabel = (string)($__briefProgress['level_name'] ?? 'Learning Stage');
    $__briefStageRank = isset($__mleBriefStageOrder[$__briefStageKey]) ? (int)$__mleBriefStageOrder[$__briefStageKey] : 0;
    $__mleBriefTotals['pending'] += $__briefPending;
    $__mleBriefTotals['scored'] += $__briefScored;
    $__mleBriefTotals['draw_cycles'] += $__briefDrawCycles;
    if ($__briefStageRank > $__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage_rank']) {
        $__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage_rank'] = $__briefStageRank;
        $__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage'] = $__briefStageLabel;
        $__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage_lottery'] = $__briefCleanName;
    }
    /* [[MLE_V5_CANONICAL_ACTION_STAGE_SYNC_2026_05_31]]
       Use the same action-state contract as the full lottery card: pending runs
       are in progress, recommended tests are action-ready, no scored evidence
       starts evidence, and no current pending runs means a fresh prediction is
       needed. Learning stage remains separate from user action state. */
    $__briefHasRecommendedAction = (!empty($__briefBatch['ready']) || !empty($__briefBatch['is_ready']) || !empty($__briefSettings['has_advice']));
    $__briefActionState = mylottoexpertResolveUserActionState($__briefPending, $__briefHasRecommendedAction, $__briefScored, $__briefChecked, $__briefMethodTrack);
    $__briefType = (string)($__briefActionState['type'] ?? 'action');
    $__briefLabel = (string)($__briefActionState['label'] ?? 'Run Next Prediction');
    $__briefDetail = (string)($__briefActionState['detail'] ?? 'Create fresh saved runs for the next draw.');
    if ($__briefLabel === 'Action Ready' && (!empty($__briefBatch['ready']) || !empty($__briefBatch['is_ready']))) {
        $__briefDetail = 'A SKAI Precision Focus batch is ready to create.';
    }
    $__briefPriority = (int)($__briefActionState['priority'] ?? (600 + $__briefScored));

    $__briefActionTarget = 'mle-section-' . $__briefLid . '-do-this-now';
    $__briefActionPanel = '';
    $__briefActionCta = (string)($__briefActionState['cta'] ?? 'Open recommended action');
    $__briefTargetKind = (string)($__briefActionState['target_kind'] ?? 'run_next');
    if ($__briefTargetKind === 'pending') {
        $__briefActionTarget = 'mle-section-' . $__briefLid . '-visual';
        $__briefActionPanel = 'mle-section-body-' . $__briefLid . '-upcoming';
    } elseif ($__briefTargetKind === 'recommended') {
        $__briefActionTarget = 'mle-section-' . $__briefLid . '-next-settings-run';
    }
    $__mleBriefCards[] = array(
        'lottery_id'=>$__briefLid,
        'name'=>$__briefName,
        'type'=>$__briefType,
        'label'=>$__briefLabel,
        'detail'=>$__briefDetail,
        'method_label'=>$__briefMethodLabel,
        'method_role'=>$__briefMethodRole,
        'method_track'=>$__briefMethodTrack,
        'stage'=>$__briefStageLabel,
        'draw_cycles'=>$__briefDrawCycles,
        'scored'=>$__briefScored,
        'pending'=>$__briefPending,
        'action_target'=>$__briefActionTarget,
        'action_panel'=>$__briefActionPanel,
        'action_cta'=>$__briefActionCta,
        'priority'=>$__briefPriority,
        'draw_cycles_label'=>mylottoexpertBriefDrawCycleLabel($__briefDrawCycles, 20),
        'draw_cycles_title'=>mylottoexpertBriefDrawCycleTitle($__briefDrawCycles, 20),
        'progress_pct'=>mylottoexpertBriefDrawCyclePercent($__briefDrawCycles, 20),
    );
}
$__mleBriefTotals['active'] = count($__mleBriefCards);
if ($__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage_lottery'] === '' && !empty($__mleBriefCards)) {
    $__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage_lottery'] = $__mleBriefCleanLotteryName((string)($__mleBriefCards[0]['name'] ?? ''));
}
/* [[MLE_V5_STRICT_PENDING_TOTAL_2026_06_01]]
   Override $__mleBriefTotals['pending'] with the sum from the strict DB map so
   the hero and momentum totals reflect only actual saved runs with identity,
   not mixed selector-derived counts. */
$__mleStrictPendingTotal = 0;
foreach ($__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId as $__mleStrictPendingVal) { $__mleStrictPendingTotal += max(0, (int)$__mleStrictPendingVal); }
$__mleBriefTotals['pending'] = $__mleStrictPendingTotal;
unset($__mleStrictPendingVal, $__mleStrictPendingTotal);
/* [[MLE_V5_STRICT_PENDING_DEBUG_PANEL_2026_06_01]]
   Temporary Super User-only diagnostics for strict pending reconciliation. */
$__mleShowStrictPendingDiagnosticPanel = (!$user->guest && $user->authorise('core.admin'));
$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticRows = array();
$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticStrictTotal = 0;
$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticTopTotal = 0;
if ($__mleShowStrictPendingDiagnosticPanel) {
    foreach ($__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId as $__mleStrictDbgLid => $__mleStrictDbgCount) {
        $__mleStrictDbgScope = $__mleStrictPendingScopeByLotteryId[(int)$__mleStrictDbgLid] ?? array();
        $__mleStrictDbgTopDisplayed = 0;
        $__mleStrictDbgName = 'Lottery #' . (int)$__mleStrictDbgLid;
        foreach ($__mleBriefCards as $__mleStrictDbgCard) {
            if ((int)($__mleStrictDbgCard['lottery_id'] ?? 0) !== (int)$__mleStrictDbgLid) { continue; }
            $__mleStrictDbgTopDisplayed = (int)($__mleStrictDbgCard['pending'] ?? 0);
            $__mleStrictDbgName = trim((string)($__mleStrictDbgCard['name'] ?? '')) !== '' ? (string)$__mleStrictDbgCard['name'] : $__mleStrictDbgName;
            break;
        }
        $__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticRows[] = array(
            'lottery_id'                 => (int)$__mleStrictDbgLid,
            'lottery_name'               => (string)$__mleStrictDbgName,
            'upcoming_draw_date'         => (string)($__mleStrictDbgScope['date'] ?? ''),
            'strict_pending_count'       => (int)$__mleStrictDbgCount,
            'top_card_pending_displayed' => (int)$__mleStrictDbgTopDisplayed,
            'pending_delta'              => (int)$__mleStrictDbgCount - (int)$__mleStrictDbgTopDisplayed,
        );
        $__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticStrictTotal += max(0, (int)$__mleStrictDbgCount);
        $__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticTopTotal += max(0, (int)$__mleStrictDbgTopDisplayed);
    }
    echo '<!-- mle-strict-pending-debug:' . json_encode($__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticRows) . ':end -->';
    unset($__mleStrictDbgLid, $__mleStrictDbgCount, $__mleStrictDbgScope, $__mleStrictDbgCard, $__mleStrictDbgTopDisplayed, $__mleStrictDbgName);
}
unset($__mleBriefSourceCards, $__briefCardRaw, $__briefLid, $__briefName, $__briefStats, $__briefSummary, $__briefVisual, $__briefProgress, $__briefBatch, $__briefSettings, $__briefTopMethod, $__briefMethodKey, $__briefMethodLabel, $__briefMethodRole, $__briefMethodTrack, $__briefPending, $__briefScored, $__briefDrawCycles, $__briefStageKey, $__briefStageLabel, $__briefStageRank, $__briefCleanName, $__briefType, $__briefLabel, $__briefDetail, $__briefPriority, $__briefActionTarget, $__briefActionPanel, $__briefActionCta, $__briefActionState, $__briefTargetKind, $__briefHasRecommendedAction);
unset($__mleStrictPendingByLotteryId, $__mleStrictPendingScopeByLotteryId, $__mleStrictPendingFullByLotteryId);
usort($__mleBriefCards, static function($a, $b) { return ((int)($b['priority'] ?? 0)) <=> ((int)($a['priority'] ?? 0)); });

$__mleBriefTopCards = $__mleBriefCards;
$__mleBriefFocusText = 'Your strategy process is ready. Open a lottery card to build evidence, score completed runs, or follow the next recommended step.';
$__mleBriefFocusTarget = '';
$__mleBriefFocusSection = '';
$__mleBriefHighestStageLabel = trim((string)$__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage']);
$__mleBriefHighestStageLottery = trim((string)$__mleBriefTotals['highest_stage_lottery']);
$__mleBriefHighestStageDisplay = $__mleBriefHighestStageLabel;
if ($__mleBriefHighestStageLottery !== '') {
    $__mleBriefHighestStageDisplay .= ' - ' . $__mleBriefHighestStageLottery;
}
$__mleBriefFocusPanel = '';
$__mleBriefFocusCta = 'Open recommended action';
if (!empty($__mleBriefTopCards)) {
    $__firstBrief = $__mleBriefTopCards[0];
    $__mleBriefFocusTarget = '#mle-adv-card-' . (int)($__firstBrief['lottery_id'] ?? 0);
    $__mleBriefFocusSection = (string)($__firstBrief['action_target'] ?? '');
    $__mleBriefFocusPanel = (string)($__firstBrief['action_panel'] ?? '');
    $__mleBriefFocusCta = (string)($__firstBrief['action_cta'] ?? 'Open recommended action');
    if ((int)($__firstBrief['pending'] ?? 0) > 0) {
        $__mleBriefFocusText = 'Focus first on ' . (string)($__firstBrief['name'] ?? 'this lottery') . ': ' . (int)$__firstBrief['pending'] . ' pending run' . ((int)$__firstBrief['pending'] === 1 ? '' : 's') . ' are waiting for the next official draw result.';
    } elseif ((string)($__firstBrief['label'] ?? '') === 'Action Ready') {
        $__mleBriefFocusText = 'Focus first on ' . (string)($__firstBrief['name'] ?? 'this lottery') . ': a recommended settings or Precision Focus action is ready.';
    } elseif ((string)($__firstBrief['label'] ?? '') === 'Start Evidence') {
        $__mleBriefFocusText = 'Focus first on ' . (string)($__firstBrief['name'] ?? 'this lottery') . ': open SKAI and create regular saved runs to build the evidence base.';
    } elseif ((string)($__firstBrief['label'] ?? '') === 'Run Next Prediction') {
        $__mleBriefFocusText = 'Focus first on ' . (string)($__firstBrief['name'] ?? 'this lottery') . ': no runs are pending for the next draw, so create fresh saved runs now.';
    } else {
        $__mleBriefFocusText = 'Focus first on ' . (string)($__firstBrief['name'] ?? 'this lottery') . ': review its current evidence stage and next recommended step.';
    }
}
$__mleHeroUserName = trim((string)($user->name ?? ''));
if ($__mleHeroUserName === '') { $__mleHeroUserName = 'member'; }
$__mleHeroFocusCta = $__mleBriefFocusCta !== '' ? $__mleBriefFocusCta : 'Open recommended action';
?>

<?php if (!empty($__mleShowStrictPendingDiagnosticPanel)): ?>
<section class="mle-strict-pending-diagnostic-panel" role="region" aria-label="Super User pending diagnostics" style="max-width:1260px;margin:14px auto 10px;padding:12px 14px;border:1px solid #f59e0b;background:#fffbea;border-radius:10px;">
  <div style="font-size:13px;font-weight:700;color:#7c2d12;margin-bottom:6px;">Temporary Super User Diagnostic Panel: Strict Pending Reconciliation</div>
  <div style="font-size:12px;color:#7c2d12;margin-bottom:10px;">
    Strict pending total: <strong><?php echo (int)$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticStrictTotal; ?></strong>
    | Top card pending total: <strong><?php echo (int)$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticTopTotal; ?></strong>
    | Delta: <strong><?php echo (int)$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticStrictTotal - (int)$__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticTopTotal; ?></strong>
  </div>
  <div style="overflow-x:auto;">
    <table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:12px;">

          <th style="text-align:left;padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fdba74;">Lottery</th>

          <th style="text-align:left;padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fdba74;">Strict pending map</th>
          <th style="text-align:left;padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fdba74;">Top card pending</th>
          <th style="text-align:left;padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fdba74;">Delta</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
      <?php if (!empty($__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticRows)): ?>
        <?php foreach ($__mleStrictPendingDiagnosticRows as $__mleStrictDiagRow): ?>
          <tr>
            <td style="padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fed7aa;"><?php echo htmlspecialchars((string)($__mleStrictDiagRow['lottery_name'] ?? ''), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></td>
            <td style="padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fed7aa;"><?php echo (int)($__mleStrictDiagRow['lottery_id'] ?? 0); ?></td>
            <td style="padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fed7aa;"><?php echo htmlspecialchars((string)($__mleStrictDiagRow['upcoming_draw_date'] ?? ''), ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8'); ?></td>
            <td style="padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fed7aa;"><?php echo (int)($__mleStrictDiagRow['strict_pending_count'] ?? 0); ?></td>
            <td style="padding:6px;border-bottom:1px solid #fed7aa;"><?php echo (int)($__mleStrictDiagRow['top_card_pending_displayed'] ?? 0); ?></td>

          </tr>
        <?php endforeach; unset($__mleStrictDiagRow); ?>
      <?php else: ?>
        <tr><td colspan="6" style="padding:6px;">No strict pending rows were collected.</td></tr>
      <?php endif; ?>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
</section>
<?php endif; ?>

<div class="mle-today-briefing" role="region" aria-label="Your Next Best Action">
  <div class="mle-today-briefing__inner">
    <div class="mle-today-briefing__top">
      <div>
        <div class="mle-today-briefing__eyebrow">Your Next Best Action</div>
        <h3 class="mle-today-briefing__title">What should I do next?</h3>
        <p class="mle-today-briefing__subtitle">
